channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Oyo State Modernises Land Ownership Through C-of-O Digitalisation
item.description: For Oyo State, governance dey take proper turn as digitalisation don enter land administration. Olusegun Olayiwola, wey don serve for grassroots to state level, don show say with correct planning, gov...

item.title: Patient awarded George Medal after preventing hospital bombing in Leeds
item.description: Nathan Newby, a 35-year-old patient from Leeds, prevented a major terrorist attack at St James’ Hospital in 2023 by calmly engaging with the man planning the bombing. Mohammad Farooq, a self-radicalis...

item.title: RCMP execute search warrants at homes of former Calgary mayor and city councillor amid ongoing investigation
item.description: The Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) have carried out search warrants at the homes of former Calgary mayor Jyoti Gondek and a current city councillor as part of an investigation that began in Octo...

item.title: Julie Oseko appointed as new National Land Commission vice-chairperson
item.description: The National Land Commission (NLC) has appointed Julie Oseko as its new vice-chairperson following a competitive internal selection process approved by the commissioners. Ms Oseko, who has served with...

item.title: Delhi HC Dismisses Lalu Prasad's Petition Against CBI FIR in Land-for-Jobs Case
item.description: The Delhi High Court delivered a major setback to Rashtriya Janata Dal (RJD) chief and former Railway Minister Lalu Prasad on 24 March 2026 by refusing to quash the CBI's FIR in the alleged land-for-r...

item.title: Hill-Lewis and Dyonase to contest DA federal leadership at April congress
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) has confirmed that Cape Town mayor Geordin Hill-Lewis and Sibusiso Dyonase will compete for the party’s top position of federal leader at its national congress in April. A...

item.title: UK proposes international effort to reopen the Strait of Hormuz amid Iran conflict and global oil disruption
item.description: The United Kingdom has offered to host an international security summit aiming to develop a collective plan for reopening the Strait of Hormuz, a vital maritime passageway through which about one-fift...

item.title: Lynx Photograph Wins Wildlife Photographer of the Year People's Choice Award
item.description: The winner of the 2026 Wildlife Photographer of the Year People's Choice award has been announced, with over 60,000 entries and public voting determining the top image. The winning photo, chosen from ...

item.title: Inflation slows slightly in February before global economic disruptions
item.description: Australia’s inflation rate slowed to 3.7 per cent in February, marking a slight easing after months of consistent increases. This moderation comes ahead of global economic instability triggered by the...

item.title: Deportations Expose Deep Rift Between Chinese Contractors in Nairobi Rail Project
item.description: This piece by columnist Jaindi Kisero dives into the recent deportation of two Chinese employees from a firm involved in a major railway station project in Nairobi. While it might seem like a simple i...

item.title: Ricky Rai's lawyer denies involvement in drifting Lamborghini incident
item.description: Ricky Rai, the son of the late underworld figure Muthappa Rai, has been accused of being behind the wheel of a Lamborghini seen performing dangerous drifting stunts on Bengaluru’s roads. The viral vid...

item.title: Family of hiker who died in B.C. mountain fall faces high medical costs
item.description: A tragic accident near Lions Bay, British Columbia, has left the family of a Chinese hiker grappling with significant emotional and financial hardship. The hiker, a Chinese national visiting Canada, s...

item.title: Wandayi blames distribution challenges for fuel shortage despite adequate national stocks
item.description: The recent fuel shortage in Kenya has caused long queues at petrol stations across several towns, sparking concern among motorists and business owners. Many filling stations have run dry, leading to p...

item.title: Karen Mthethwa takes over mid-morning show as YFM announces 2026 line-up changes
item.description: Youth-focused radio station YFM has rolled out major updates to its 2026 line-up, reflecting its ongoing commitment to showcasing young, dynamic talent. Radio personality Karen Mthethwa, who first joi...

item.title: Australia's Housing Targets Behind Schedule Due to Global Disruptions
item.description: Australia is facing delays in meeting its ambitious housing targets due to a combination of factors, including the Middle East conflict. The country set a goal of building 1.2 million new homes by 202...

item.title: Philippines Seeks US Waivers for Oil Importation Amid Energy Crisis
item.description: The Philippines has sought the assistance of the United States in obtaining oil from countries under US sanctions, as it faces a growing energy crisis due to the ongoing Middle East conflict. Jose Man...

item.title: Pakistan proposes to host potential US-Iran peace negotiations in Islamabad
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has officially offered Islamabad as a venue for possible negotiations between the United States and Iran to help end the ongoing conflict in West Asia. The initiative, an...

item.title: Oil prices drop while Asian markets rise amid speculation over US-Iran negotiations
item.description: Oil prices dropped by more than 5% on Wednesday, largely due to growing hopes for a de-escalation of tensions between the United States and Iran. Asian stock markets, including Japan’s Nikkei 225, Sou...

item.title: How Nigerian Politicians Use Festivals and Gatherings for Political Moves
item.description: Last weekend, Nigerians dey celebrate Eid el-Fitri, but e no just be celebration as na time politicians dey plan dem next moves for next year election. E don dey common for politicians to hide their w...

item.title: Reflections on Kay Scarpetta and the Evolution of Crime Fiction
item.description: Dr Kay Scarpetta, the fictional chief medical examiner of Virginia, was introduced in Patricia Cornwell’s 1990 crime novel 'Postmortem', inspired by Cornwell’s own experiences as a police reporter and...
